title: The Western Cape #ClassOf2024 in numbers
description: LOCAL NEWS - A total of 75 647 candidates...
bodyText:
LOCAL NEWS - A total of 75 647 candidates will write the National Senior Certificate (NSC) exams this year in the Western Cape.

Of these, 64 375 are full-time candidates, and 11 272 are part-time candidates.Western Cape Minister of Education, David Maynier, says 270 more full-time candidates are writing this year compared to the November matric exams in 2023.


> The exams begin on Monday 21 October with 66 988 candidates writing English Home Language, First Additional Language, or Second Additional Language in the morning sitting.


In total, 123 examination papers will be written before the exams end on Wednesday 27 November.Maynier says as is the case every year, the subject with the most candidates writing is Mathematical Literacy, with 51 932 candidates writing Paper 1 on Friday 1 November 2024, and Paper 2 on Monday 4 November.“In contrast, five subjects have a single candidate writing: Zulu Home Language, Sepedi Home Language, Sepedi First Additional Language, Setswana First Additional Language and Hebrew Second Additional Language,” Maynier says.


> The exams will be written at 474 exam centres, overseen by 1 809 invigilators who will keep an eye out for any procedural irregularities.


Marking will take place between 2 and 12 December, with 920 000 examination scripts being marked by 4 232 markers and checked by 1 046 mark-checkers.

The national results will be announced on 15 January, with individual candidate’s results available at schools or online the following day.

Photo: @davidmaynier on X

Maynier says the scale of the matric exams poses a mammoth administrative challenge. “We appreciate the tremendous efforts of the Western Cape Education Department’s (WCED) officials and school staff in ensuring that the exams run smoothly and fairly.


> “Our matrics, and their teachers, have put so much work into preparing for these exams, so we appeal to everyone in the Western Cape to make our matrics the VIPs of our province in these coming weeks.


“Let us all play our part by supporting our matrics during what is understandably a stressful period, so that they can do the very best they can!”

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
